title: My Jean!
content:
Tune - "The Northern Lass."


        Though cruel fate should bid us part,
            Far as the pole and line,
        Her dear idea round my heart,
            Should tenderly entwine.
        Though mountains rise, and deserts howl,
            And oceans roar between;
        Yet, dearer than my deathless soul,
            I still would love my Jean
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "My Jean!"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
